description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a slipper lobster, characterized by its broad, flattened body and rough, mottled exoskeleton with shades of brown, orange, and dark spots. The carapace is robust and covers the cephalothorax, with a segmented abdomen that tapers toward a fan-shaped tail. The legs are short and sturdy, with six visible on each side, and the antennae are short and paddle-like, positioned at the front. The eyes are small and set close together on the anterior part of the carapace. The surface appears hard and textured, with a matte, slightly rough finish typical of marine crustaceans.
description: A broad, mottled slipper lobster with a hard, rough exoskeleton, short legs, and a fan-shaped tail. Its body is segmented and features short, paddle-like antennae.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, segmented abdomen, and all legs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the broad, flat carapace, short antennae, and closely set eyes.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the segmented body and legs, with the tail fanned out.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail fan and the tapering of the abdomen, with legs partially visible.
